label: Russia invades Ukraine
description: Russian forces enter Ukraine. European gas markets enter sustained crisis; TTF surges from ~80 to over 200 EUR/MWh within weeks. EU begins emergency storage mandate discussions.

label: Nord Stream indefinite shutdown
description: Gazprom halts Nord Stream 1 flows indefinitely, citing turbine maintenance. TTF peaks near 350 EUR/MWh. Germany declares gas emergency. EU begins demand reduction measures.

label: EU storage mandate enters force
description: EU Regulation 2022/1032 requires member states to fill gas storage to 90% by November 1 each year. Structural floor placed under injection-season demand.

label: Ukraine-Russia transit agreement expires
description: Five-year Ukraine-Russia gas transit agreement expires. Remaining Russian pipeline volumes via Ukraine cease. Small residual flows to Slovakia, Austria, Hungary affected.

label: US tariff threats on EU imports
description: US administration signals broad tariffs on European goods. Market concern over retaliatory measures affecting US LNG export agreements and cargo diversion.

label: Norwegian Asgard/Troll maintenance
description: Scheduled maintenance on Norwegian Asgard transport system and Troll field reduces Norwegian pipeline export capacity by approximately 15% for three weeks.

label: Cold spell: Northern Europe
description: Sustained cold weather across Scandinavia and Northern Europe drives heating demand above seasonal norms. Norwegian hydro reservoir drawdown accelerates.

label: US tariff escalation
description: US formalises 25% tariffs on European steel and aluminium. EU considers counter-measures including LNG offtake reviews. TTF reacts to uncertainty over US LNG cargo commitments.

label: Norwegian Troll outage
description: Unplanned outage at Troll field reduces Norwegian gas exports by approximately 50 mcm/day for ten days. One of the largest unplanned reductions since 2022.

label: Operation Epic Fury — US/Israel strikes on Iran
description: US and Israeli forces conduct strikes on Iranian nuclear and military infrastructure. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed. TTF surges ~40% in 72 hours. Markets immediately reprice Strait of Hormuz transit risk. Goldman Sachs and IEA begin modelling LNG supply disruption scenarios.

label: Iranian drones strike Ras Laffan LNG
description: Iranian drone strike on QatarEnergy's Ras Laffan industrial complex. Approximately 17% of Qatar's 77 Mt/yr LNG export capacity taken offline. QatarEnergy declares force majeure. Multi-year repair timeline confirmed. Largest LNG supply disruption since 2022 Russian shock.

label: Strait of Hormuz officially closed
description: Iran formally closes the Strait of Hormuz to all non-Iranian vessel traffic. Approximately 20% of global LNG trade transits the strait. JKM-TTF spread collapses as Asian cargoes cannot reroute to Europe. LNG insurance premiums increase from 0.125% to 0.2–0.4% per transit.

label: Qatar LNG diversion
description: QatarEnergy diverts three surviving LNG cargoes from Europe to Asia following JKM-TTF spread widening. European LNG sendout falls 12% week-on-week. Gate and Dunkirk terminal utilisation drops sharply.

label: Ras Laffan damage — 3–5 year repair confirmed
description: QatarEnergy confirms structural damage to two LNG trains at Ras Laffan; repair timeline 3–5 years. Implies structural loss of ~20 Mt/yr LNG supply to Europe through 2027–2028. EU storage injection season begins with reserves at ~30%, lowest since 2022. ECB revises 2026 inflation projections upward.

label: EU storage mandate confirmed at 90%
description: EU regulation extended requiring member states to reach 90% storage fill by November 1. Post-Hormuz LNG disruption means injection targets are at serious risk without demand-side measures or US LNG diversions.

label: Hormuz ceasefire — first truce
description: Fragile ceasefire announced following US-Iran diplomatic contacts. Strait partially reopens. TTF pulls back from peak of approximately €70/MWh. Markets remain cautious; no restoration of Ras Laffan export capacity.

label: US naval blockade of Iranian ports
description: US announces naval blockade of Iranian export terminals as part of dual-pressure strategy. Effectively prevents any Iranian crude or gas condensate exports. Geopolitical premium re-enters TTF as blockade risks escalation.

label: Strait of Hormuz reopens under truce
description: Iran reopens the Strait under a monitored truce agreement. Commercial LNG transit resumes under US naval escort. JKM-TTF spread widens modestly as Asian cargoes compete for Qatari volumes. European gas markets remain structurally undersupplied for the 2026 injection season.

label: Strait of Hormuz re-closed
description: Iran re-closes the Strait following breakdown of truce talks. TTF re-rallies. EU energy ministers convene emergency session to assess injection-season risk. Demand-side measures and US LNG diversion programmes accelerated.
